obligations.text: Message intake captures body-to-last-flag and latches the attribute word.
obligations.trigger: input stream offers a new message while IP is idle
obligations.guarantee: all body beats to last-flag captured; dest EID/tag/TO/msg-type latched
obligations.observable: input_ready handshake and latched message context

obligations.text: Body is fragmented into MTU-bounded packets with a possibly-short final packet.
obligations.trigger: a non-empty captured message is ready to emit
obligations.guarantee: packet count = ceil(body_len/MTU); each payload <= MTU; only last may be short
obligations.observable: egress packet payload lengths and packet count

obligations.text: SOM/EOM mark message boundaries correctly including single-packet case.
obligations.guarantee: SOM only on first packet, EOM only on last; single packet sets both

obligations.text: Packet sequence number starts at 0 and increments mod 4 across a message.
Header field composition, ownership, and byte ordering are correct.
obligations.guarantee: header fields per ownership rules in DSP0236 byte layout; body order preserved
obligations.observable: egress header bytes and body bytes

obligations.text: Exactly one message is processed at a time with no packet interleaving.
obligations.trigger: input stream offers a new message while one is in flight
obligations.guarantee: new message not accepted until in-flight message emits EOM
obligations.observable: input_ready gating and per-packet message identity

obligations.text: Backpressure stalls; underrun/oversize/zero-length flagged and counted; no partial emit.
obligations.trigger: backpressure, underrun, oversize, or zero-length condition
obligations.guarantee: stall without loss; error flag+counter on abort conditions; no partial message emitted
obligations.observable: error flag, error counter, suppressed egress

obligations.text: Done/error interrupts and sent/dropped counters reflect message outcomes.
obligations.guarantee: done IRQ on EOM, error IRQ on error, counters updated, CSR-visible
obligations.observable: done_irq, error_irq, sent/dropped counters via CSR

obligations.text: Reset/default observable state is known and mechanically checkable.
obligations.trigger: async reset assert then synchronous deassert
obligations.guarantee: idle, seq=0, counters cleared, CSR fields at reset defaults
obligations.observable: busy flag, seq, counters, CSR reset values

variables.input_valid: source asserts a body beat is valid

assume.env: Source presents attribute_word on the first beat and input_last on the final beat.

guarantee.dut: Every beat with input_valid and input_ready is captured; context latched from the first beat.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show captured context equals behavior_model.tx.intake for every scenario row, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

variables.body_len: captured message body length in bytes
variables.mtu_cfg: configured maximum packet payload in bytes

assume.env: mtu_cfg is stable while a message is in flight; body_len > 0.

guarantee.dut: packet_count == ceil(body_len/mtu_cfg) and each payload <= mtu_cfg with only the final packet short.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show packet count and payload lengths equal behavior_model.tx.fragment, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

assume.env: packet_count derives from the fragmentation contract.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show SOM/EOM equal behavior_model.tx.som_eom for every packet, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

variables.prev_seq: previous packet sequence number in this message

assume.env: packets of one message are emitted in order.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show sequence numbers equal cycle_rules.tx.seqnum across a multi-packet message, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

assume.env: caller dest_eid/to/tag/msg_type stable for the message; source_eid CSR programmed before intake.

guarantee.dut: header_bytes equal the DSP0236 layout of the composed fields and body bytes preserve input order.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show header byte vector and body order equal behavior_model.tx.header, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

assume.env: source may offer a second message at any time.

guarantee.dut: input_ready for a new message stays low until the cycle after eom_emitted; packets never interleave.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show no interleaving and intake gating equal cycle_rules.tx.singlemsg, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

variables.egress_valid: DUT drives a valid egress beat

assume.env: egress_ready may deassert at any time; malformed inputs (underrun/oversize/zero-length) may occur.

guarantee.dut: backpressure stalls with no byte lost; underrun/oversize/zero-length set error_flag and increment error_counter; no partial message is emitted.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show stall-without-loss and error flag/counter equal behavior_model.tx.err for each negative scenario, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

guarantee.dut: done_irq pulses on EOM emission, error_irq pulses on error, and sent/dropped counters track outcomes.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show IRQ pulses and counter values equal behavior_model.tx.status, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

assume.env: reset asserted asynchronously and deasserted synchronously to the clock.

guarantee.dut: after synchronous deassertion busy_flag=0, packet_seq_num=0, error_counter=0, CSR fields at reset defaults.

contracts.pass_condition: scoreboard_rows.v1 rows show post-reset observable state equals behavior_model.tx.reset, zero failed rows
contracts.implementation_policy: TB language and simulator are not fixed by this contract

requirements.text: The IP shall accept one outbound MCTP message on a streaming input bus where a last-flag marks the final body beat, accompanied by a sideband attribute word carrying destination EID, message tag, TO bit, and message type/IC; the message body is treated as opaque payload.

The IP shall fragment the accepted message body into a sequence of MCTP packets whose per-packet payload never exceeds the configured MTU; the final packet may be shorter than the MTU; a message that fits in one packet shall produce exactly one packet.
The IP shall set SOM=1 on the first packet of a message and EOM=1 on the last packet; a single-packet message shall have both SOM=1 and EOM=1; all other packets shall have SOM=0 and EOM=0.
The IP shall set the 2-bit packet sequence number to 0 on the SOM packet and increment it modulo 4 on each subsequent packet of the same message.

Each emitted packet shall carry a transport header with a fixed header version, destination EID, TO, and message tag passed through from the caller, source EID from a CSR, and IP-computed SOM/EOM/sequence number; the header shall be emitted in DSP0236-defined byte layout and the body bytes in the order received.
The IP shall process one message at a time; it shall accept a new message only after the current message has fully emitted its EOM packet, with no interleaving of packets from different messages.

On egress backpressure the IP shall stall emission without dropping data; on source underrun (premature termination), oversize message, or a zero-length message it shall raise the corresponding status flag and increment the error counter; the IP shall never emit a partial message to egress.

The IP shall raise a per-message done interrupt when a message's EOM packet has been emitted, raise an error interrupt on any error condition, and maintain sent-message and dropped-message counters, all accessible over an APB-like CSR interface.

The IP shall use an asynchronously-asserted, synchronously-deasserted active-low reset; while reset is asserted and immediately after deassertion the IP shall be idle with no in-flight message, packet sequence number 0, error counters cleared, and all CSR fields at their defined reset values.

execution_policy.notes: Signoff requires oag.check and oag.decide; authoring packets are work inputs, not closure decisions.
